data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Adobe.com likely utilizes a microservices architecture to handle its vast array of services and functionalities efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Deployed using Docker and Kubernetes for scalability and management.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Use of a CDN to deliver content quickly to users around the globe.
data.technical_spec: Integrated with Akamai or similar CDN for fast content delivery.

data.title: Scalable Database System
data.description: High-performance databases to manage and store user data and content efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Likely uses a combination of SQL and NoSQL databases like MySQL and MongoDB.

data.title: Redundant Server Architecture
data.description: Redundant server systems to ensure high availability of services.
data.technical_spec: Servers are likely located in multiple regions for failover and disaster recovery.

data.title: Edge Computing
data.description: Implemented edge computing to process data closer to the user source.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes edge nodes for processing to enhance performance.
